food_subgroup: Deep-fried fish snack / Indian street food

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Fish Koliwada. Mapped to the closest defensible parent class 'fried fish food product' (FOODON_03315595). Exact term for this Indian preparation is absent from FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Fish, battered or breaded, fried) is the closest defensible match. Fish Koliwada uses a besan (gram flour) batter with Indian spices; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for this specific preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Fish Koliwada or equivalent Indian fried fish snack as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Fish Koliwada is approximately 150g, equivalent to 4–6 bite-sized fried fish pieces, as commonly served as a street food snack or appetizer in Mumbai.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fish Koliwada is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for fried fish preparations and besan-battered fried snacks, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) entries for fried fish and chickpea flour. Nutrient values represent a composite estimate for this preparation. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central fried fish data (FDC ID 175168). Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, cholesterol, and water data not available in ICMR-NIN for this specific preparation; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — chickpea flour and fried fish analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Fish Koliwada. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous foods from the University of Sydney GI Database: besan (chickpea flour) batter has a GI of approximately 35–45; fried fish has negligible carbohydrate contribution to GI. The dominant carbohydrate source is gram flour (besan), which is a low-GI legume flour. High protein content from fish further attenuates postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (45 × 8.0 / 100) × 1.5 = 5.4g, classified as low GL. Estimation is defensible and consistent with published GI values for besan-based preparations.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (380mg per 100g; ~570mg per 150g serving) from seasoning and deep-frying process
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Fish Koliwada to occasional consumption and request reduced-salt preparation, as a single 150g serving provides approximately 25% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and low carbohydrate content (8g per 100g) are favorable, but high fat from deep-frying contributes to caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fish Koliwada has a low GI and moderate carbohydrate load, making it a relatively acceptable protein-rich snack for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions (one 150g serving), though the high fat content from deep-frying warrants caloric monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying increases total fat and potentially oxidized lipid content; however, fish provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Fish Koliwada should be consumed sparingly due to its high total fat (14g/100g) and sodium (380mg/100g) from deep-frying, though the underlying fish provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids; grilled or baked preparation would be a healthier alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fish provides anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids, but deep-frying may generate pro-inflammatory oxidized lipids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Fish Koliwada provides omega-3 fatty acids from fish that may benefit rheumatoid arthritis, but deep-frying can introduce pro-inflammatory compounds; consumption should be moderate and ideally replaced with grilled fish preparations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Koliwada is a classic Mumbai street food snack originating from the Koli fishing community of coastal Maharashtra, India. It consists of bite-sized pieces of fish — commonly pomfret, surmai (kingfish), or white-fleshed fish — marinated in a spiced batter of gram flour (besan), ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, carom seeds (ajwain), lemon juice, and salt, then deep-fried in hot oil until golden and crunchy. The dish is characterized by its vibrant red-orange color, crispy exterior, and tangy-spicy flavor profile. It is typically served with lemon wedges and green mint chutney as a street food snack, appetizer, or bar accompaniment. Nutritionally, Fish Koliwada provides approximately 220 kcal per 100g, with 15.5g protein, 14g fat, and 8g carbohydrate. The high protein content from fish supports satiety and muscle maintenance. The gram flour batter contributes a low glycemic index (~45), making it relatively favorable for blood sugar management compared to wheat-flour-battered alternatives. However, deep-frying significantly increases fat content and caloric density, and the sodium content (380mg/100g) is a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Fish Koliwada is a good source of vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), niacin (4.5mg/100g), selenium (25mcg/100g), and phosphorus (180mg/100g), reflecting the nutritional profile of the underlying fish. It is not suitable for those with fish allergies and should be consumed in moderation by individuals managing weight, hypertension, or cardiovascular conditions.

confidence_reason: Fish Koliwada is not listed as a discrete entry in any single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for fried fish and besan-battered preparations, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried fish entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for besan-based foods. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach rather than a direct source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175168, fish fried in batter) as no discrete Fish Koliwada entry exists in approved sources. Saturated fat (2.8g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central fried fish data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile and moisture data for this specific Indian besan-battered fried fish preparation are not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for besan (chickpea flour) batter preparations; no direct GI measurement for Fish Koliwada exists in published literature. 4. Portions in the input JSON contained three identical duplicate entries (150g); deduplicated to a single canonical serving of 150g. 5. This is an Indian dish; multilingual naming requirements apply for Hindi (satisfied). Chinese, Malay, Tamil local names are not applicable for this Indian Maharashtrian dish and are set to null. 6. Marathi alias फिश कोळीवाडा included in flat aliases array for retrieval coverage.
